data.narrative:
Zap Energy is a Series D nuclear energy company founded by Benj Conway, Brian A. Nelson, and Uri Shumlak, currently valued at $1.0B post-money following $330M+ in total capital raised. The valuation assessment draws on a comparable universe of 11 peers—3 public companies (OKLO, NNE, SMR) and 8 private firms—operating in the same sector and valuation tier, including TerraPower ($4.0B), X-Energy ($2.0B), Kairos Power ($1.6B), and Nano Nuclear Energy ($1.5B).

Two methods triangulate forward value. Stage-progression analysis applies a typical 1.5x multiplier from Series D to the next funding stage, yielding $1.5B. Peer-median valuation across 6 same-sector comparables with similar scale produces $1.6B, with the peer set spanning $200M to $4.0B. The median peer valuation of $1.6B reflects the maturity and capital intensity of the nuclear technology subsector.

Triangulation across both methods supports a valuation range of $1.3B (low) to $1.8B (high), with a median of $1.5B. This implies modest upside from the current $1.0B post-money valuation, consistent with typical Series D progression. Key uncertainties include regulatory pathway clarity, technology demonstration timeline, and capital requirements for commercialization—factors with material impact on relative positioning within the peer cohort.
